violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were added at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Chicken wings were observed held at 110 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken wings were reheated to above 165 DF at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

ROP salmon was observed thawing in the prep sink with the ROP packaging still intact. Salmon was observed at 35 DF at the time of inspection. Salmon was removed from the packaging and placed in the walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The floor in the kitchen storage area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. The establishment employees individuals with the required certification, but does not have anyone registered with the state. Establishment is register an individual with the DOH. New Violation. Correct By: 08/25/2023

Fish, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Establishment voluntarily disposed of the fish. Fish will be cooled uncovered moving forward. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Chicken Wings was observed held at 125 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrective action- wings were heated up to 165 D F. The hot hold unit temperature was increased before putting the chicken back inside. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
The floor in the kitchen back storage area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2024
